SCENE-CONDITIONED MARITIME PoL — 1 · retrospective anomalies · 2 · live threat assessment · 3 · multi-hour behaviors · 4 · play-forward behaviors
Scene-Conditioned Maritime Pattern-of-Life · v2 · Causal / Forward-Looking

Live threat assessment — Hampton Roads / lower Chesapeake

Replay of 6 hours of the held-out region, Hampton Roads / lower Chesapeake Bay (June 7, 2024, 12:00–18:00 UTC, 360 tracks). Every minute, each vessel's most recent 12 minutes are scored against the model's expectation given only its past — no future context, exactly as a live system would run. Thresholds are calibrated against the live decision rule itself (≤5% of benign tracks per 6-hour watch). Toggle the conditioning: place-only (v2), or place + recognized behavior (demo 03/04 classes) — at the same benign budget, working vessels earn headroom (threshold 1.02×), transit vessels tighten (0.88×), and two new alert kinds appear: motion inconsistent with recognized behavior and the compound unrecognized behavior + kinematic surprise. Model frozen; the port was never trained on.

CONDITIONING
12:00Z
nominal elevated (≥0.85×thr) ALERT (≥thr) compound: unknown behavior + kinematic transit-state change (info) off chart / warming up click a vessel or an alert card
Contact
Click a vessel on the map, or an alert in the feed.
Alert feed 0
Behavior-conditional assessment (new): the long-timescale arm (demos 03/04) labels every vessel's multi-hour behavior; here those labels condition the alert threshold, recalibrated on benign traffic at the same ≤5% watch budget: working 1.02× (weave is normal for them), transit 0.88× (their anomalies surface earlier), station 0.94×; unknown/warming-up falls back to 1.00. On this watch: 15 place-only alerts → 29 conditioned alerts (0 removed, 14 added — mostly long-dormant vessels now maneuvering, i.e. motion inconsistent with recognized behavior; 2 compound). Honest caveats: the trailing 8.5-h label lags activity changes (some "inconsistency" alerts are routine departures — a two-timescale behavior state is the refinement), and place-conditioning already absorbs most working-vessel weirdness, so conditioning buys sensitivity more than suppression.
What changed vs v1 (the locked-in retrospective analysis): v1 masked a span in the middle of each window — conditioning on past and future, fine for forensics, impossible live. v2 masks the trailing span (in-distribution: training placed spans at random positions) and re-tests each vessel every minute. That re-testing is why the threshold must be calibrated against the operational rule, not per-window: ~300 highly-overlapping tests per track turn a per-window 5% FAR into alarms on 44% of benign tracks. Calibrating the track-level watch FAR instead (quantile search on sliding benign traffic) yields 15 alerts in 6 h here — a display a watchstander can actually use. Get-underway/anchoring transitions and off-chart spans are shown as informational states, not threats.
MarineCadastre AIS (Jun 2024) · GMRT bathymetry + USACE charted channels (scene tokens) · span-mask TrajBERT-style scorer, scene-aware arm, frozen; trained on Puget Sound + SF Bay only · causal trailing-span scoring · CPU-only · public data.
